data.content:
# RunsOn: Self-Hosted GitHub Actions Runners on AWS

RunsOn is a modern self-hosted GitHub Actions runner service that reduces CI/CD costs by 7-15x while delivering 30% better CPU performance compared to GitHub's official runners. It deploys entirely within your AWS account, providing full control over infrastructure, security, and costs. The system uses ephemeral EC2 instances that spin up for each job and automatically terminate when complete, with support for spot instances (automatic fallback to on-demand), warm pools for faster startup times, and integrated S3-based caching that's up to 5x faster than GitHub's default cache.

RunsOn consists of three main components: a Go-based server running on AWS App Runner that manages the EC2 fleet and GitHub integration, a lightweight agent that bootstraps on each EC2 instance to execute jobs, and CloudFormation templates (MIT licensed) that define all AWS infrastructure. The system supports Linux (x64/arm64), Windows, and GPU runners across multiple AWS availability zones and environments. Key features include spot pricing with automatic fallback, multi-AZ support, SSH access to runners, static IPs for private networking, automatic cost reporting, and compatibility with existing GitHub Actions workflows through simple label changes.

## CloudFormation Stack Deployment

Deploy the complete RunsOn infrastructure using AWS CloudFormation with all required AWS resources including VPC networking, App Runner service, IAM roles, S3 buckets, SQS queues, and DynamoDB tables.

```bash
# Basic deployment with embedded networking (new VPC created)
aws cloudformation create-stack \
  --stack-name runs-on-prod \
  --region us-east-1 \
  --template-url https://runs-on.s3.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/cloudformation/template.yaml \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM \
  --parameters \
    ParameterKey=GithubOrganization,ParameterValue=my-org \
    ParameterKey=LicenseKey,ParameterValue="arn:aws:ssm:us-east-1:123456789:parameter/my-stack/license-key" \
    ParameterKey=EmailAddress,ParameterValue=ops@example.com \
    ParameterKey=NetworkingStack,ParameterValue=embedded

# Advanced deployment with custom configuration
aws cloudformation create-stack \
  --stack-name runs-on-advanced \
  --region us-east-1 \
  --template-url https://runs-on.s3.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/cloudformation/template.yaml \
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM \
  --parameters \
    ParameterKey=GithubOrganization,ParameterValue=my-org \
    ParameterKey=LicenseKey,ParameterValue="arn:aws:ssm:us-east-1:123456789:parameter/license" \
    ParameterKey=EmailAddress,ParameterValue=ops@example.com \
    ParameterKey=NetworkingStack,ParameterValue=embedded \
    ParameterKey=Private,ParameterValue=true \
    ParameterKey=AppCPU,ParameterValue=512 \
    ParameterKey=AppMemory,ParameterValue=1024 \
    ParameterKey=RunnerDefaultDiskSize,ParameterValue=60 \
    ParameterKey=RunnerMaxRuntime,ParameterValue=720 \
    ParameterKey=EnableDashboard,ParameterValue=true \
    ParameterKey=EnableEfs,ParameterValue=true \
    ParameterKey=SpotCircuitBreaker,ParameterValue="3/20/45" \
    ParameterKey=AlertTopicSlackWebhookUrl,ParameterValue="https://hooks.slack.com/services/XXX/YYY/ZZZ"

# Check deployment status
aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
  --stack-name runs-on-prod \
  --region us-east-1 \
  --query "Stacks[0].{Status:StackStatus,Outputs:Outputs}"

# Get service endpoint and configuration
aws cloudformation describe-stacks \
  --stack-name runs-on-prod \
  --region us-east-1 \
  --query "Stacks[0].Outputs[?OutputKey=='RunsOnEntryPoint' || OutputKey=='RunsOnEgressStaticIps'].[OutputKey,OutputValue]" \
  --output table
```

## GitHub Actions Workflow Integration

Replace standard GitHub runners with RunsOn by changing the runs-on label to use dynamic runner configuration with custom CPU, memory, storage, and instance family specifications.

```yaml
# Basic usage - replace ubuntu-latest
jobs:
  build:
    # Before: runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    runs-on: runs-on,runner=2cpu-linux-x64,env=production
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: npm test

# CPU and memory intensive build
jobs:
  heavy-build:
    runs-on: runs-on,cpu=8,ram=32,family=c7a,env=production
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: cargo build --release

# GPU-accelerated workflow
jobs:
  ml-training:
    runs-on: runs-on,family=g4dn.xlarge,image=ubuntu22-gpu-x64,env=production
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: python train.py --gpu

# Custom storage configuration with extras
jobs:
  large-build:
    runs-on: runs-on,runner=2cpu-linux-x64,volume=100gb:gp3:750mbps:4000iops,extras=s3-cache+efs+tmpfs,env=production
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3
      - run: docker build -t myapp .

# Warm pool usage for fast startup
jobs:
  quick-test:
    runs-on: runs-on,pool=small-x64,env=dev
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: npm test

# Matrix build with deterministic grouping
jobs:
  test:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        node: [18, 20, 22]
    runs-on: runs-on=${{ github.run_id }},cpu=4,ram=8,env=production
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node }}
      - run: npm test

# Private networking with static IPs
jobs:
  secure-deploy:
    runs-on: runs-on,runner=2cpu-linux-x64,private=true,env=production
    steps:
      - run: curl https://internal-api.company.com/deploy
```

## Runner Configuration File

Define custom runner specifications, AMI images, and warm pools using the .github/runs-on.yml configuration file in your repository.

```yaml
# .github/runs-on.yml

# Custom AMI images
images:
  # GPU-enabled deep learning AMI
  dlami-x64:
    platform: "linux"
    arch: "x64"
    owner: "898082745236"
    name: "Deep Learning Base OSS Nvidia Driver GPU AMI (Ubuntu 22.04)*"

  # Custom Ubuntu AMI with preinstall script
  custom-ubuntu:
    owner: "099720109477"
    name: "ubuntu/images/hvm-ssd/ubuntu-jammy-22.04-amd64-server-*"
    arch: x64
    platform: linux
    preinstall: |
      #!/bin/bash
      apt-get update
      apt-get install -y docker.io kubectl
      touch /tmp/preinstalled

# Named runner configurations
runners:
  # Fast C7 instance with custom tags
  fast-build:
    image: ubuntu22-full-x64
    cpu: 4
    family: [c7a, c7i]
    preinstall: |
      #!/bin/bash
      echo "Setting up build environment"
      npm install -g pnpm
    tags:
      - "team:platform"
      - "cost-center:engineering"

  # GPU runner for ML workloads
  gpu-nvidia:
    family: ["g4dn.xlarge"]
    image: dlami-x64
    preinstall: |
      #!/bin/bash
      nvidia-smi
      echo "GPU ready"

  # Memory-optimized with S3 cache
  memory-intensive:
    cpu: 8
    ram: 64
    family: [r7a]
    image: ubuntu24-full-x64
    extras: s3-cache+efs
    tags:
      - "workload:data-processing"

# Warm pools for fast startup (<10s for hot, ~20s for stopped)
pools:
  # Hot pool - instances stay running
  small-x64:
    env: production
    runner: fast-build
    timezone: "America/New_York"
    schedule:
      - name: business-hours
        match:
          day: ["monday", "tuesday", "wednesday", "thursday", "friday"]
          time: ["08:00", "18:00"]
        stopped: 0
        hot: 5
      - name: off-hours
        match:
          day: ["monday", "tuesday", "wednesday", "thursday", "friday"]
          time: ["18:00", "08:00"]
        stopped: 2
        hot: 1
      - name: weekends
        match:
          day: ["saturday", "sunday"]
        stopped: 2
        hot: 0

  # Stopped pool - instances warmed then stopped
  dev-pool:
    env: dev
    runner: fast-build
    timezone: "Europe/Paris"
    schedule:
      - name: default
        stopped: 3
        hot: 0
```

## Job Label System

Control runner configuration dynamically through GitHub Actions job labels including CPU, RAM, storage, instance family, and features.

```yaml
# Available label parameters:
# - runs-on=<group>         # Deterministic grouping (use github.run_id)
# - runner=<name>           # Named runner from runs-on.yml
# - cpu=<count>             # CPU cores (1-96)
# - ram=<gb>                # Memory in GB (2-384)
# - family=<type>           # EC2 instance family (c7a, m7a, r7a, g4dn, etc.)
# - image=<name>            # AMI image name
# - volume=<spec>           # Storage: <size>:<type>:<throughput>:<iops>
# - pool=<name>             # Warm pool name
# - env=<environment>       # Environment filter (production, dev, staging)
# - private=<bool>          # Use private networking
# - extras=<features>       # Additional features (s3-cache, efs, tmpfs, ecr-cache)

jobs:
  # Minimal configuration
  minimal:
    runs-on: runs-on,env=production

  # Named runner reference
  use-named:
    runs-on: runs-on,runner=fast-build,env=production

  # Custom CPU and memory
  custom-specs:
    runs-on: runs-on,cpu=16,ram=64,env=production

  # Specific instance family
  compute-optimized:
    runs-on: runs-on,family=c7a.4xlarge,env=production

  # Custom storage configuration
  large-storage:
    runs-on: runs-on,cpu=4,volume=200gb:gp3:1000mbps:5000iops,env=production

  # Multiple extras combined
  full-featured:
    runs-on: runs-on,cpu=8,ram=16,extras=s3-cache+efs+tmpfs+ecr-cache,env=production

  # Warm pool with fast startup
  pool-job:
    runs-on: runs-on,pool=small-x64,env=production

  # GPU with specific image
  gpu-job:
    runs-on: runs-on,family=g4dn.xlarge,image=ubuntu22-gpu-x64,env=production

  # Private networking with static IP
  secure-job:
    runs-on: runs-on,cpu=2,private=true,env=production

  # Matrix builds with grouping
  matrix:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        version: [1, 2, 3]
    runs-on: runs-on=${{ github.run_id }},cpu=4,ram=8,env=production
```

## CloudFormation Parameters Reference

Configure the RunsOn stack deployment with parameters controlling networking, compute resources, monitoring, and integrations.

```yaml
# Key parameters for cloudformation/template.yaml

# REQUIRED PARAMETERS
GithubOrganization: "my-org"              # GitHub organization name (case-sensitive)
LicenseKey: "arn:aws:ssm:region:account:parameter/path"  # License from runs-on.com/pricing
EmailAddress: "ops@example.com"            # Alert email address

# NETWORKING
NetworkingStack: "embedded"                # "embedded" (new VPC) or "external" (existing VPC)
Private: false                            # Private networking mode (no public IPs on runners)
Ipv6Enabled: false                        # Enable IPv6
SSHAllowed: false                         # Allow SSH access to runners
SSHCidrRange: "0.0.0.0/0"                 # CIDR range for SSH access
VpcCidrBlock: "10.1.0.0/16"               # VPC CIDR for embedded networking
NatGatewayAvailability: 3                 # NAT gateways per AZ (1-3)

# COMPUTE RESOURCES
AppCPU: 256                               # App Runner CPU (256-4096)
AppMemory: 512                            # App Runner memory in MB (512-8192)
RunnerDefaultDiskSize: 40                 # Default EBS volume size in GB
RunnerLargeDiskSize: 120                  # Large disk size in GB
RunnerMaxRuntime: 720                     # Max job runtime in minutes
SpotCircuitBreaker: "2/15/30"             # "interruptions/window_minutes/backoff_minutes"

# MONITORING & ALERTS
EnableDashboard: false                    # CloudWatch dashboard
OtelExporterEndpoint: ""                  # OpenTelemetry endpoint (Grafana, SignalOz)
ServerPassword: ""                        # Password for /metrics endpoint
CostReportsEnabled: true                  # Daily cost reports
AlertTopicSlackWebhookUrl: ""             # Slack webhook for alerts
Ec2LogRetentionInDays: 7                  # EC2 instance log retention

# FEATURES
EnableEfs: false                          # Enable EFS for shared storage
EnableEphemeralRegistry: false            # Ephemeral ECR registry
EncryptEbs: false                         # Encrypt EBS volumes
S3CacheExpirationInDays: 10               # S3 cache TTL

# ADVANCED
Environment: "production"                 # Environment name
AppGithubApiStrategy: "normal"            # "normal" or "patient" (rate limit handling)
RunnerConfigAutoExtendsFrom: ""           # Base configuration to extend
CostAllocationTag: ""                     # AWS cost allocation tag
RunnerCustomTags: ""                      # Custom EC2 tags (JSON)
```

## Development and Testing

Build, test, and deploy RunsOn using Make commands for local development, staging releases, and production deployments.

```bash
# Prerequisites
# - Go 1.21+ installed via mise
# - Docker with buildx
# - AWS CLI configured with appropriate profiles

# Update submodules (includes proprietary server code)
make pull

# Server development (requires .env file in server/ directory)
cd server && make lint              # Run golangci-lint
cd server && make test              # Run unit tests with race detection
cd server && make agent             # Build agent binaries (Linux arm64/x64, Windows x64)
cd server && make server            # Build server binary

# Run local development server
make dev-run                        # Start server with hot reload
make dev-run fast                   # Skip agent rebuild
make dev-warns                      # Show warnings from dev logs

# Build and push development release
make dev                            # Build Docker image, push to registry, update S3 template

# Install development stack
make dev-install                    # Deploy CloudFormation stack with dev template

# View development stack info
make dev-show                       # Show stack outputs (entry point, service ARN, etc.)
make dev-logs                       # Stream App Runner logs
make dev-logs-instances             # Stream EC2 instance logs

# Query DynamoDB job data
make dev-get-job 123456789          # Get job details by job ID
make dev-get-instance i-0abc123     # Get EC2 instance details

# Run smoke tests
make dev-smoke                      # Trigger and wait for GitHub workflow

# Staging release process
make check                          # Verify version consistency
make stage                          # Build, tag, and push staging release
make promote                        # Promote staging to production

# Testing (requires LICENSE_KEY environment variable)
make test-install-embedded          # Test embedded networking
make test-install-external          # Test external networking
make test-smoke                     # Run smoke tests against test stack
make test-delete                    # Delete test stack and cleanup

# CloudFormation template preparation
./scripts/prepare-template.sh dev registry:tag version
./scripts/prepare-template.sh stage registry:tag version

# Trigger spot interruption for testing
make trigger-spot-interruption i-0abc123 i-0def456

# Stream local logs to CloudWatch for dashboard testing
make dev-stream-logs                # Run in separate terminal while dev-run active
```

## Monitoring and Observability

Access metrics, logs, and cost data through CloudWatch, Prometheus endpoints, and integrated monitoring tools.

```bash
# Access Prometheus metrics endpoint (requires ServerPassword)
curl -u admin:your-server-password https://your-stack-url.com/metrics

# CloudWatch Logs
# App Runner logs: /aws/apprunner/RunsOnService-{id}/{id}/application
# EC2 logs: /aws/runs-on/{StackName}/ec2

# View logs using awslogs CLI
awslogs get --aws-region us-east-1 /aws/apprunner/RunsOnService-xxx/yyy/application -w -s 10m --timestamp
awslogs get --aws-region us-east-1 /aws/runs-on/runs-on-prod/ec2 -w -s 10m --timestamp

# CloudWatch Dashboard (if EnableDashboard=true)
# Automatically created nested stack: {StackName}-DashboardStack-{id}

# OpenTelemetry integration
# Set OtelExporterEndpoint parameter to Grafana Cloud or SignalOz endpoint
# Format: https://otlp-gateway.grafana.net/otlp
# Set OtelExporterHeaders for authentication

# Cost tracking via AWS Cost Explorer
# Cost allocation tag: runs-on:stack-name={StackName}
# Daily cost reports sent via email and Slack (if CostReportsEnabled=true)

# SSH into runner (requires SSHAllowed=true, proper key setup)
aws ssm start-session --target i-0abc123 --reason "debugging"

# Get runner metadata from within job
echo "Instance ID: $RUNS_ON_INSTANCE_ID"
echo "Job ID: $RUNS_ON_JOB_ID"
echo "Stack: $RUNS_ON_STACK_NAME"
echo "ECR Cache: $RUNS_ON_ECR_CACHE"
echo "EFS Mount: /mnt/efs"

# CloudWatch alarms
# - App Runner CPU/Memory utilization
# - SQS queue age (default: 300s threshold)
# - EC2 instance failures
# - Spot interruption rate

# Slack alert webhook configuration
AlertTopicSlackWebhookUrl: "https://hooks.slack.com/services/XXX/YYY/ZZZ"

# HTTPS webhook for custom integrations
AlertTopicSubscriptionHttpsEndpoint: "https://your-endpoint.com/alerts"
```

## Advanced Features and Integrations

Leverage S3 caching, EFS shared storage, ECR ephemeral registry, and custom networking for advanced CI/CD workflows.

```yaml
# S3-based Actions cache (5x faster than GitHub default)
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: runs-on,runner=2cpu-linux-x64,extras=s3-cache,env=production
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.npm
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-npm-${{ hashFiles('package-lock.json') }}
      - run: npm install
      - run: npm test

# EFS shared storage for artifact sharing between jobs
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: runs-on,cpu=4,extras=efs,env=production
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: cargo build --release
      - run: cp target/release/myapp /mnt/efs/myapp

  test:
    needs: build
    runs-on: runs-on,cpu=2,extras=efs,env=production
    steps:
      - run: /mnt/efs/myapp --test

# Ephemeral ECR registry for Docker layer caching
jobs:
  docker-build:
    runs-on: runs-on,cpu=4,extras=ecr-cache,env=production
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3
      - uses: docker/build-push-action@v4
        with:
          context: .
          push: true
          tags: ${{ env.RUNS_ON_ECR_CACHE }}:myapp:latest
          cache-from: type=registry,ref=${{ env.RUNS_ON_ECR_CACHE }}:myapp:buildcache
          cache-to: type=registry,ref=${{ env.RUNS_ON_ECR_CACHE }}:myapp:buildcache,mode=max

# tmpfs for fast I/O
jobs:
  io-intensive:
    runs-on: runs-on,cpu=8,ram=32,extras=tmpfs,env=production
    steps:
      - run: |
          # /home/runner, /tmp, and /var/lib/docker on tmpfs
          df -h /home/runner
          df -h /tmp

# Private networking with NAT gateway egress
jobs:
  secure-build:
    runs-on: runs-on,cpu=4,private=true,env=production
    steps:
      - run: |
          # No public IP, egress via NAT gateway with static IP
          curl https://api.github.com

# GitHub Enterprise Server support
# Set GithubEnterpriseUrl parameter:
# GithubEnterpriseUrl: "https://github.mycompany.com"

# Custom IAM policies for runners
# EC2InstanceCustomPolicy: "arn:aws:iam::123456789:policy/MyCustomPolicy"
# AppCustomPolicy: "arn:aws:iam::123456789:policy/MyAppPolicy"

# Permission boundaries
# DefaultPermissionBoundaryArn: "arn:aws:iam::123456789:policy/OrgBoundary"

# Step Security integration (security scanning)
# IntegrationStepSecurityApiKey: "arn:aws:ssm:us-east-1:123456789:parameter/stepsec-key"

# Custom EC2 tags (JSON format)
# RunnerCustomTags: '[{"Key":"team","Value":"platform"},{"Key":"project","Value":"ci"}]'
```

## RunsOn is a comprehensive solution for organizations seeking cost-effective, performant, and fully self-hosted GitHub Actions runners. The CloudFormation-based deployment model ensures infrastructure consistency and repeatability, while the flexible job label system allows developers to specify exact compute requirements per workflow. The integrated S3 caching, warm pools, and spot instance support with automatic fallback provide significant cost savings without compromising reliability. Use cases span from basic CI/CD pipelines requiring simple compute resources to complex workflows demanding GPU acceleration, large memory footprints, or specialized networking configurations.

Integration patterns follow a progressive enhancement approach: start with basic runner replacement using simple labels, then layer on advanced features like warm pools for frequently-run jobs, S3 caching for dependency management, and EFS for cross-job artifact sharing. The system's architecture separates infrastructure management (CloudFormation), orchestration (Go server on App Runner), and execution (agents on EC2), enabling independent scaling and updates. Organizations can deploy multiple environments (dev, staging, production) in isolated stacks, use custom AMIs with preinstall scripts for specialized toolchains, and integrate with existing monitoring solutions through OpenTelemetry and CloudWatch. The MIT-licensed templates and comprehensive parameter system make RunsOn adaptable to diverse organizational requirements while maintaining the proprietary server/agent components for core functionality.
